title: Buffer Overflow, Underflow, and Copy Boundaries · Undefined-Behavior Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted bounded frame transformation, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded frame transformation and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.
Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.
Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Off By One Proof
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 4167. Produce bounded C evidence to move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Fortify Runtime Limits
steps.instruction:
For this debug demonstration of cmem-fortify-runtime-limits, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-fortify-runtime-limits-1j5630 */" for changed case 7474. It must move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails.
